message.content: Stage 1 complete ✅. All artifacts validated. Advancing to Stage 2.

function.arguments: {"command": "plan", "stage_list": [{"id": "S1", "name": "Requirements + Spec + Padframe + Advisory", "status": "done", "owner": "mcu_factory_requirements", "notes": "Completed. 17 IPs linked, soc_config.yaml confirmed 512KB flash, 128KB SRAM, 1 ADC, 3 I2C. Advisory (Spec Advisory) run, output at docs/advisory/spec_advisory.md."}, {"id": "S2", "name": "Verification Planning", "status": "in_progress", "owner": "mcu_factory_verification_planning", "notes": "Build checklist + scenario YAMLs from requirements/spec/soc_config."}, {"id": "S3", "name": "Generation (nc_chipgen generate)", "status": "planned", "owner": "mcu_factory_generation", "notes": "Run nc_chipgen generate with all scenario files."}, {"id": "S4", "name": "Verification Execution (nc_chipgen verify)", "status": "planned", "owner": "mcu_factory_verification", "notes": "Run nc_chipgen verify --all --coverage."}, {"id": "S5", "name": "Verification Review + Dashboard + Advisory", "status": "planned", "owner": "mcu_factory_verification_reviewer", "notes": "Review evidence, update checklist, run MCU Factory Verification Review Advisory."}, {"id": "S6", "name": "Physical Design (PnR)", "status": "planned", "owner": "chipgen_pnr", "notes": "Place-and-route, GDS generation, signoff."}]}
function.name: stage_tracker

function.arguments: {"agent": "mcu_factory_verification_planning", "task": "Build a complete verification checklist and generate all scenario YAMLs for the MSPM33C321A derivative MCU (mspm33c321a_deriv).", "inputs": {"project_root": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526", "design_name": "mspm33c321a_deriv", "requirements_path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/requirements.md", "spec_path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/specifications.md", "padframe_requirements_path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/padframe_requirements.md", "mcu_architecture_path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/mcu_architecture.md", "soc_config_path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/config/soc_config.yaml", "design_profile": {"cpu": "hazard3_2port_swd (RISC-V RV32I)", "clock_hz": 80000000, "flash_kb": 512, "sram_kb": 128, "package": "LQFP64", "gpio_pins": 48, "uart_count": 4, "spi_count": 2, "i2c_count": 3, "tmr_count": 6, "pwm_count": 2, "adc_count": 1, "dmac_channels": 8, "additional_peripherals": ["CRC", "AES", "WWDT", "RTC", "SYSTICK"]}, "required_outputs": ["/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/dv/verification_checklist.md", "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/dv/verification_checklist.html", "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/docs/dv/scenario_manifest.yaml", "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/"], "acceptance_criteria": ["Every checklist item must include a scenario name", "Scenario YAML files must cover all checklist-required tests", "Checklist and scenario_manifest.yaml must be consistent \u2014 every checklist scenario name maps to an existing YAML file", "Both .md and .html versions of the checklist must be produced"]}}
function.name: delegate_to_agent

content:
Delegated agent finished with result:

mcu_factory_verification_planning finishes task with status: completed, summary: 
## Verification Planning Complete — mspm33c321a_deriv

### What was done
All six mandatory workflow steps were executed successfully for the MSPM33C321A derivative MCU (`mspm33c321a_derivative`).

### Deliverables

#### 1. Checklist Markdown
**Path:** `docs/dv/verification_checklist.md`
- 97 checklist items across 18 domains
- Every item includes: `[ID]`, requirement text, `Test:`, `Priority:`, `Scenario:`
- Priority breakdown: **P0=63**, **P1=32**, **P2=3**

#### 2. Checklist HTML
**Path:** `docs/dv/verification_checklist.html`
- Interactive filterable table (filter by domain, priority, free-text search)
- Color-coded priority and status badges
- Derivative-delta items (I2C2 — new instance per R-006) flagged with `DERIV` badge
- Domain summary table at bottom
- 60 KB self-contained HTML with embedded CSS and JS

#### 3. Scenario YAML Files
**Directory:** `verification/scenarios/` — **21 files generated**

| Scenario File | Domain | Items Covered |
|---|---|---|
| scenario_sys_reset_clock.yaml | SYS | SYS-01..05 |
| scenario_xip_flash.yaml | XIP | XIP-01..04 |
| scenario_sram_memory.yaml | MEM | MEM-01..04 |
| scenario_gpio_basic.yaml | GPIO | GPIO-01..07 |
| scenario_gpio_af_irq.yaml | GPIO/IRQ | GPIO-08,09, IRQ-05 |
| scenario_uart_loopback.yaml | UART | UART-01..04 |
| scenario_uart_advanced.yaml | UART | UART-05..08 |
| scenario_spi_transfer.yaml | SPI | SPI-01..05 |
| scenario_i2c_transfer.yaml | I2C | I2C-01..08 (all 3 instances incl. new I2C2) |
| scenario_timer_basic.yaml | TMR | TMR-01..07 |
| scenario_pwm_output.yaml | PWM | PWM-01..04 |
| scenario_dma_transfer.yaml | DMA | DMA-01..07 |
| scenario_crc_kat.yaml | CRC | CRC-01..04 |
| scenario_aes_kat.yaml | AES | AES-01..06 |
| scenario_wwdt_window.yaml | WWDT | WWDT-01..04 |
| scenario_rtc_calendar.yaml | RTC | RTC-01..05 |
| scenario_systick_timer.yaml | SYSTICK | SYSTICK-01..03 |
| scenario_irq_fabric.yaml | IRQ | IRQ-01..04 |
| scenario_swd_debug.yaml | DBG | DBG-01..04 |
| scenario_integration_boot.yaml | INT | INT-01 |
| scenario_integration_multiperiph.yaml | INT | INT-02..05 |

Every scenario YAML includes: `scenario_name`, `description`, `checklist_ids`, `test_names`, `priority`, `dut` (filelist), `clock`, `reset`, `external_devices` (all with required `type` field), `stimulus` steps, `checks`, and `pass_criteria`.

#### 4. Scenario Manifest
**Path:** `docs/dv/scenario_manifest.yaml`
- `scenarios` section: 21 entries with name, file, checklist_ids, test_names, priority, domain
- `coverage_map` section: 97 entries — one per checklist item
- `metadata` section: totals, priority breakdown, derivative delta items, out-of-scope notes

### Quality Gate Results
- ✅ Checklist markdown exists with `Scenario:` on every item — **97/97**
- ✅ Checklist HTML exists with interactive filter/search
- ✅ 21 scenario YAMLs generated under `verification/scenarios/` — all files exist on disk
- ✅ Manifest maps all 97 checklist IDs to scenario names — **0 gaps**
- ✅ All 21 manifest `file:` paths resolve to existing YAML files
- ✅ Bidirectional ID check: no IDs in checklist missing from manifest, and no IDs in manifest missing from checklist
- ✅ `I2C-04` explicitly covers I2C2 — the derivative-addition instance (R-006)
- ✅ `external_devices` entries all include required `type` field
- ✅ ID regex `[A-Z0-9]+-\d+` used throughout (handles I2C-, SYSTICK- prefixes correctly)
- ✅ No simulations executed (planning stage only)

### Derivative-Specific Coverage Highlights
- **I2C2 (new instance, R-006):** Covered by `I2C-04` / `test_i2c2_transfer` in `scenario_i2c_transfer.yaml` with dedicated i2c2_slave_model. Also covered in `I2C-08` smoke test.
- **512 KB Flash (R-003):** XIP-04 boundary test explicitly checks 0x0007FFFF / 0x00080000 limit.
- **128 KB SRAM (R-004):** MEM-02 full 128 KB address walk, MEM-04 concurrent CPU+DMA.
- **Single ADC (R-005):** Documented as out-of-scope (mixed-signal block outside NC_SOCGEN RTL); noted in manifest metadata.
, checklist_md: docs/dv/verification_checklist.md, checklist_html: docs/dv/verification_checklist.html, scenarios_dir: verification/scenarios/, scenario_count: 21, manifest: docs/dv/scenario_manifest.yaml, total_checklist_items: 97, priority_P0: 63, priority_P1: 32, priority_P2: 3, coverage_gaps: 0, domains: 18, derivative_items_flagged: ['I2C-04', 'I2C-08'], next_steps: 
The verification planning bundle is complete and ready for the simulation/evaluation stage. Recommended next steps:

1. **RTL Generation (nc_chipgen):** Run NC_SOCGEN with `config/soc_config.yaml` to generate `rtl/compute_ss/` RTL and filelists referenced in all scenario YAMLs.
2. **Firmware Build:** Compile `verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex` against the generated linker script and memory map (used by scenario_xip_flash and scenario_integration_boot).
3. **BFM Smoke Tests (Tier 1):** Run NC_SOCGEN-generated BFM testbenches for fast register smoke coverage (XIP-02, UART-08, SPI-05, I2C-08, TMR-07, PWM-04, CRC-04).
4. **Cocotb Integration Tests (Tier 2):** Implement and run cocotb tests for each scenario using the scenario YAMLs as the specification. Use the external device models (uart, i2c_memory, spi_slave, gpio_stimulus).
5. **Coverage Closure:** Track coverage per domain; merge and report. Target 100% P0 items passing before tape-out.
6. **Dashboard Update:** Populate `docs/dv/verification_dashboard.html` (to be created by evaluator) with per-item pass/fail status.

outputs.next_steps:
The verification planning bundle is complete and ready for the simulation/evaluation stage. Recommended next steps:

1. **RTL Generation (nc_chipgen):** Run NC_SOCGEN with `config/soc_config.yaml` to generate `rtl/compute_ss/` RTL and filelists referenced in all scenario YAMLs.
2. **Firmware Build:** Compile `verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex` against the generated linker script and memory map (used by scenario_xip_flash and scenario_integration_boot).
3. **BFM Smoke Tests (Tier 1):** Run NC_SOCGEN-generated BFM testbenches for fast register smoke coverage (XIP-02, UART-08, SPI-05, I2C-08, TMR-07, PWM-04, CRC-04).
4. **Cocotb Integration Tests (Tier 2):** Implement and run cocotb tests for each scenario using the scenario YAMLs as the specification. Use the external device models (uart, i2c_memory, spi_slave, gpio_stimulus).
5. **Coverage Closure:** Track coverage per domain; merge and report. Target 100% P0 items passing before tape-out.
6. **Dashboard Update:** Populate `docs/dv/verification_dashboard.html` (to be created by evaluator) with per-item pass/fail status.
